question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + HashLib.isLeaf (Merkle second-preimage via hashNode)] Can an attacker construct a proof where an internal node hash equals a valid leaf hash, allowing a forged proof to pass isLeaf, under the precondition that hashNode does not distinguish leaf from internal nodes (no domain separation), triggering the call sequence: attacker finds internal_node_hash == hashOffer(fake_offer) -> constructs proof using internal node as leaf -> isLeaf traverses tree using internal node as leaf -> returns true for wrong offer -> isRatified passes for unauthorized offer, violating the invariant that only offers explicitly included in the signed Merkle tree must pass isLeaf, causing scoped impact: unauthorized offer fills that could freeze legitimate offer slots via consumption group exhaustion? Proof idea: attempt to construct a second-preimage proof and assert isLeaf rejects it.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (offer.group consumption and cancelRoot interaction)] Can an attacker cancel a root after partially filling offers in a group, freezing the remaining capacity of the group, under the precondition that a consumption group has multiple offers under one root and the root is canceled after partial fills, triggering the call sequence: taker partially fills offer_A in group G -> attacker cancels root -> remaining offers in group G become unfillable via EcrecoverRatifier -> consumed[maker][G] is partially consumed but no new fills possible, violating the invariant that a consumption group's remaining capacity must be accessible until explicitly exhausted or all offers expire, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of remaining group capacity, blocking repayment flows? Proof idea: state-transition test with partial fill followed by cancelRoot and assert subsequent fills revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.claimContinuousFee (fee claimer path)] Can a freeze of take paths caused by root cancellation indirectly freeze the claimContinuousFee path by preventing totalUnits from being updated correctly, under the precondition that continuousFeeCredit depends on totalUnits and take is the primary mechanism for updating totalUnits, triggering the call sequence: attacker cancels all roots -> no takes possible -> totalUnits stagnates -> continuousFeeCredit accrual is blocked -> claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver) reverts if continuousFeeCredit < amount, violating the invariant that fee claimer must always be able to claim accrued fees, causing scoped impact: long-term freeze of continuous fee claim path? Proof idea: invariant test asserting that claimContinuousFee remains callable regardless of take activity.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: cancelRoot + Midnight.withdraw (indirect freeze via credit stagnation)] Can cancellation of all maker roots in a market freeze lender withdrawals by preventing any takes that would increase withdrawable, under the precondition that the market has no repayments and all liquidity flows through EcrecoverRatifier-signed offers, triggering the call sequence: attacker cancels all roots -> no takes possible -> no repayments -> withdrawable never increases -> lenders call withdraw but withdrawable < units -> underflow revert, violating the invariant that lenders must always be able to withdraw available credit, causing scoped impact: long-term freeze of lender withdrawals until alternative offers are created? Proof idea: invariant test asserting that withdrawable is always sufficient for outstanding credit after all takes are blocked.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (offer.start boundary)] Can an offer with start > block.timestamp pass isRatified but fail the OfferNotStarted check, creating a window where the offer is signed but unfillable, under the precondition that the root is canceled during the window between signing and offer.start, triggering the call sequence: maker signs offer with start = T+1 -> attacker cancels root at T -> at T+1, taker calls take -> isRatified checks isRootCanceled -> RootCanceled() revert, violating the invariant that an offer must be fillable from its start time until its expiry, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of offers whose roots are canceled during the pre-start window? Proof idea: fuzz block.timestamp relative to offer.start and cancelRoot timing.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + HashLib.hashOffer (reduceOnly field)] Can a maker freeze their own reduce-only offers by signing with reduceOnly=true but having the offer filled in a context where buyerCreditIncrease > 0 or sellerDebtIncrease > 0, causing MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased() revert in take, under the precondition that the market state changes between signing and taking such that the reduce-only condition is violated, triggering the call sequence: maker signs offer with reduceOnly=true -> market state changes -> taker calls take -> isRatified passes -> take checks reduceOnly condition -> MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased() revert, violating the invariant that a reduce-only offer must be fillable when the maker's position is being reduced, causing scoped impact: freeze of reduce-only repayment offers when market conditions change? Proof idea: fuzz market state transitions and assert reduce-only offers remain fillable when maker position is decreasing.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: cancelRoot + Midnight.setIsAuthorized (authorized address can add authorizations)] Can an attacker who is authorized by a maker grant authorization to a malicious contract that calls cancelRoot in a callback during a take, under the precondition that isAuthorized[maker][attacker] == true and attacker can grant authorization to malicious_contract, triggering the call sequence: attacker calls setIsAuthorized(malicious_contract, true, maker) -> malicious_contract is triggered as a callback during take -> malicious_contract calls cancelRoot(maker, root) -> root canceled mid-take, violating the invariant that root cancellation must not be possible during an active take execution, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of all offers under the root, blocking subsequent repayment paths? Proof idea: PoC with callback that calls cancelRoot and assert root is canceled after take completes.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (abi.decode of ratifierData)] Can a malformed ratifierData ABI encoding cause isRatified to revert with a generic ABI decode error, permanently blocking a take, under the precondition that the taker passes ratifierData that is not valid ABI encoding of (Signature, bytes32, uint256, bytes32[]), triggering the call sequence: taker calls take(offer, malformed_ratifierData, ...) -> isRatified calls abi.decode(ratifierData, (Signature, bytes32, uint256, bytes32[])) -> reverts with ABI decode error -> take reverts, violating the invariant that invalid ratifierData must produce a clear error without corrupting state, causing scoped impact: DoS of take path for any offer if ratifierData is malformed? Proof idea: fuzz ratifierData byte sequences and assert all malformed inputs revert cleanly without state changes.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (signature malleability)] Can a signature with high-s value (s > secp256k1n/2) pass ecrecover and return a valid signer address, allowing signature malleability to create two valid signatures for the same offer, under the precondition that EcrecoverRatifier does not enforce low-s normalization, triggering the call sequence: attacker computes high-s variant of valid signature -> calls take with high-s signature -> ecrecover returns same signer -> isRatified passes -> offer is filled, violating the invariant that each offer must have exactly one canonical signature to prevent replay confusion, causing scoped impact: potential double-fill confusion if signature malleability is exploited across different ratifierData variants? Proof idea: compute both low-s and high-s signatures for same offer and assert both pass isRatified.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (cross-chain replay)] Can a signature created on chain A be replayed on chain B if both chains have the same block.chainid (e.g., testnets or forks with same chain ID), under the precondition that EcrecoverRatifier uses block.chainid in the domain separator and two chains share the same chain ID, triggering the call sequence: maker signs offer on chain A -> attacker replays signature on chain B with same chain ID -> isRatified computes same domain separator -> ecrecover returns same signer -> offer fills on chain B without maker's intent, violating the invariant that signatures must be chain-specific and non-replayable, causing scoped impact: unauthorized fills on chain B that consume maker's group budget, freezing legitimate offers? Proof idea: differential test with two Midnight deployments sharing the same chain ID and assert signature replay succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: cancelRoot + Midnight.liquidate (post-maturity liquidation path)] Can cancellation of all EcrecoverRatifier roots in a market freeze post-maturity liquidations if liquidators rely on taking offers to source repayment assets, under the precondition that the market is post-maturity and all available liquidity is in EcrecoverRatifier-signed offers, triggering the call sequence: market reaches maturity -> attacker cancels all roots -> liquidators cannot source repayment via take -> liquidate with callback fails -> unhealthy positions cannot be liquidated, violating the invariant that overdue debt after maturity must always be liquidatable, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of post-maturity liquidation path, allowing bad debt to accumulate indefinitely? Proof idea: invariant test asserting that liquidate remains callable post-maturity regardless of EcrecoverRatifier root state.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (offer.tick not multiple of tickSpacing)] Can an offer signed with a tick that is valid at signing time become permanently unfillable if the market's tickSpacing is decreased after signing, under the precondition that the offer's tick is a multiple of the old tickSpacing but not of the new tickSpacing, triggering the call sequence: market created with tickSpacing=4 -> maker signs offer with tick=4 -> tickSpacingSetter decreases spacing to 2 -> taker calls take -> tick % tickSpacing == 4 % 2 == 0 -> passes (finer ticks keep previous ticks valid) -> but if spacing decreases to 3, tick=4 fails tick % 3 != 0, violating the invariant that existing signed offers must remain fillable after tick spacing changes, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of offers at ticks that become invalid after spacing change? Proof idea: fuzz tick and tickSpacing combinations and assert that tick validity is monotonically preserved as spacing decreases.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (lossFactor maxed out)] Can a market with lossFactor == type(uint128).max permanently freeze all takes (including those using EcrecoverRatifier), under the precondition that bad debt has accumulated to the point where lossFactor reaches type(uint128).max, triggering the call sequence: bad debt liquidations push lossFactor to type(uint128).max -> any take call checks require(_marketState.lossFactor < type(uint128).max) -> MarketLossFactorMaxedOut() revert -> all EcrecoverRatifier-signed offers become permanently unfillable, violating the invariant that lenders must be able to withdraw remaining credit even after maximum loss, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of all take paths in the market, blocking any remaining withdrawal or settlement? Proof idea: fuzz bad debt accumulation and assert that lossFactor reaching type(uint128).max blocks all takes.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: cancelRoot + Midnight.claimSettlementFee (indirect freeze)] Can cancellation of all roots in a market freeze the settlement fee claim path by preventing any takes that would increase claimableSettlementFee, under the precondition that claimableSettlementFee[token] is only increased during take and all takes are blocked by root cancellation, triggering the call sequence: attacker cancels all roots -> no takes possible -> claimableSettlementFee[token] never increases -> feeClaimer calls claimSettlementFee(token, amount, receiver) -> claimableSettlementFee[token] -= amount underflows if amount > current balance, violating the invariant that the fee claimer must always be able to claim accrued settlement fees, causing scoped impact: long-term freeze of settlement fee claims? Proof idea: invariant test asserting claimableSettlementFee is always sufficient for pending claims after all takes are blocked.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (offer.market.maturity in hash)] Can a maker freeze their offers by signing with a specific maturity that differs from the market's actual maturity stored in the market struct, under the precondition that the taker passes an Offer with offer.market.maturity != actual_market.maturity, triggering the call sequence: taker calls take(offer_with_wrong_maturity, valid_ratifierData, ...) -> hashMarket includes maturity -> produces different hash -> isLeaf fails -> InvalidProof() revert, violating the invariant that offer validity must be tied to the exact market parameters, causing scoped impact: freeze of take path if market maturity is mismatched in the offer struct? Proof idea: fuzz offer.market.maturity and assert isLeaf fails for any value != signed maturity.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (offer.market.enterGate/liquidatorGate in hash)] Can a market creator freeze all EcrecoverRatifier-signed offers by creating a market with different gate addresses than what was included in the signed offer's market hash, under the precondition that the offer was signed with specific gate addresses and the market is created with different gates, triggering the call sequence: maker signs offer with market{enterGate=A, liquidatorGate=B} -> market is created with enterGate=C -> taker calls take -> hashMarket includes enterGate -> different hash -> isLeaf fails -> InvalidProof() revert, violating the invariant that offers must be fillable in the market they were signed for, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of all offers signed for a market with specific gate configuration? Proof idea: differential test comparing hashMarket output for same market with different gate addresses.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: cancelRoot + Midnight.repay (direct repay path not affected)] Can an attacker mislead a borrower into believing their repayment path is frozen by canceling roots, when in fact direct repay() without take is still available, under the precondition that the borrower has debt and the attacker cancels all EcrecoverRatifier roots, triggering the call sequence: attacker cancels all roots -> borrower believes repay is impossible -> borrower does not call repay() directly -> debt accumulates past maturity -> position becomes liquidatable, violating the invariant that borrowers must always be informed that direct repay() is independent of EcrecoverRatifier, causing scoped impact: indirect freeze of repayment via social engineering enabled by root cancellation? Proof idea: unit test asserting that repay() succeeds regardless of EcrecoverRatifier root state.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (offer.maxUnits=0 and offer.maxAssets=0)] Can an offer with both maxUnits=0 and maxAssets=0 pass the MultipleNonZero check in take but cause unexpected behavior in consumption tracking, under the precondition that the offer is signed with maxUnits=0 and maxAssets=0, triggering the call sequence: taker calls take(offer_with_both_zero, valid_ratifierData, units, ...) -> atMostOneNonZero(0, 0) returns true -> consumed[maker][group] += units -> require(newConsumed <= 0) -> ConsumedUnits() revert on first fill, violating the invariant that an offer with zero caps must not be fillable for any nonzero units, causing scoped impact: freeze of take path for zero-cap offers, blocking any repayment that relies on them? Proof idea: unit test with maxUnits=0 and maxAssets=0 and assert take reverts for any units > 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (offer.callback in hash)] Can a maker freeze their offers by including a callback address in the signed offer that is a contract that always reverts, under the precondition that offer.callback is set to a malicious contract that returns wrong value or reverts, triggering the call sequence: taker calls take(offer_with_bad_callback, valid_ratifierData, ...) -> isRatified passes -> take executes callback -> callback reverts or returns wrong value -> WrongBuyCallbackReturnValue() or WrongSellCallbackReturnValue() revert, violating the invariant that a maker must not be able to sign offers with callbacks that permanently block fills, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of all takes for offers with malicious callback addresses? Proof idea: unit test with callback that always reverts and assert take always fails.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (offer.receiverIfMakerIsSeller in hash)] Can a maker freeze their sell offers by signing with receiverIfMakerIsSeller set to a blacklisted or non-receivable address, under the precondition that the loan token has a blacklist and receiverIfMakerIsSeller is blacklisted, triggering the call sequence: taker calls take(offer_with_blacklisted_receiver, valid_ratifierData, ...) -> isRatified passes -> take calls safeTransfer(loanToken, blacklisted_receiver, sellerAssets) -> token reverts -> take reverts, violating the invariant that a maker's sell offers must be fillable regardless of receiver address validity, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of all takes for offers with non-receivable receiver addresses? Proof idea: unit test with blacklisted receiver and assert take reverts at token transfer.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: cancelRoot + Midnight.multicall (atomic cancel and state manipulation)] Can an attacker use Midnight.multicall to atomically cancel a root and perform a state-changing action that depends on the canceled root being valid, under the precondition that multicall executes calls sequentially via delegatecall, triggering the call sequence: multicall([cancelRoot_call, take_call_using_canceled_root]) -> cancelRoot succeeds -> take fails with RootCanceled() -> entire multicall reverts -> no state change, violating the invariant that multicall must not allow partial state updates when a later call fails, causing scoped impact: no freeze from this specific path, but confirms atomicity of multicall with EcrecoverRatifier? Proof idea: unit test with multicall containing cancelRoot followed by take and assert full revert on take failure.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (offer.group as bytes32 in hash)] Can two offers with the same parameters but different group values produce different leaf hashes, allowing an attacker to fill an offer under the wrong group and exhaust a different group's budget, under the precondition that the taker passes an offer with a different group than what was signed, triggering the call sequence: taker calls take(offer_with_wrong_group, valid_ratifierData_for_correct_group, ...) -> hashOffer includes group -> different hash -> isLeaf fails -> InvalidProof() revert, violating the invariant that group membership must be cryptographically bound to the signed offer, causing scoped impact: freeze of take path if group is mutated, blocking repayment flows? Proof idea: fuzz offer.group and assert isLeaf fails for any group != signed group.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (offer.tick in hash)] Can an attacker freeze a specific offer by passing a different tick value in the offer struct while providing a valid proof for the original offer, under the precondition that the taker controls the offer parameter in take(), triggering the call sequence: taker calls take(offer_with_different_tick, valid_ratifierData_for_original_tick, ...) -> hashOffer includes tick -> different hash -> isLeaf fails -> InvalidProof() revert, violating the invariant that the executed price must match the signed price, causing scoped impact: freeze of take path if tick is mutated, blocking repayment at the intended price? Proof idea: fuzz offer.tick and assert isLeaf fails for any tick != signed tick.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: cancelRoot + Midnight.take (offer.expiry near maturity)] Can an attacker cancel a root just before maturity to freeze all last-minute repayment offers, under the precondition that borrowers rely on EcrecoverRatifier-signed buy offers to repay debt near maturity, triggering the call sequence: attacker monitors mempool -> at block.timestamp = maturity - 1 -> attacker calls cancelRoot(maker, root) -> all buy offers under root become unfillable -> borrowers cannot repay via take -> debt becomes overdue at maturity -> positions become liquidatable, violating the invariant that borrowers must have a repayment path available until maturity, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of last-minute repayment paths, forcing borrowers into post-maturity liquidation? Proof idea: fuzz cancelRoot timing relative to maturity and assert repayment path availability.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take (offer.buy field in hash)] Can a taker freeze a take by flipping the offer.buy field from the signed value, under the precondition that the taker passes offer.buy = !signed_buy, triggering the call sequence: taker calls take(offer_with_flipped_buy, valid_ratifierData, ...) -> hashOffer includes buy -> different hash -> isLeaf fails -> InvalidProof() revert, violating the invariant that the direction of an offer must be cryptographically bound to the signature, causing scoped impact: freeze of take path if buy direction is mutated, blocking repayment flows that depend on specific offer direction? Proof idea: unit test flipping offer.buy and assert isLeaf fails.
